---
title: "Research Blog"
description: "Notes & tutorials on robotics, control theory, optimal control, JAX, and lessons from hardware experiments by Evanns Morales-Cuadrado."
listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date desc"
  type: default
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  feed: true
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

```{=html}
<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Crimson+Pro:ital,wght@0,400;0,600;0,700;1,400;1,600&display=swap');

#title-block-header { display: none; }

/* Hide the auto-generated Quarto listing — we keep `listing:` in YAML
   purely so Quarto still produces /blog/index.xml for RSS subscribers. */
.quarto-listing { display: none !important; }

/* ─────────────────  Research Blog tokens  ───────────────── */
:root {
  --rb-accent:       #2563eb;
  --rb-accent-soft:  #3b82f6;
  --rb-warn:         #f59e0b;
  --rb-muted:        #94a3b8;

  --rb-mono:    "Iosevka", "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  --rb-serif:   "Crimson Pro", "Source Serif Pro", Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;

  --rb-card-bg:     #eef3fb;
  --rb-card-border: #b7c1d4;

  --rb-paper-bg:     #fbfaf5;             /* warm off-white */
  --rb-paper-ink:    #1f2937;
  --rb-paper-rule:   #d6cfb5;
}

body.quarto-dark {
  --rb-accent:       #60a5fa;
  --rb-accent-soft:  #93c5fd;
  --rb-warn:         #fbbf24;
  --rb-muted:        #94a3b8;

  --rb-card-bg:     rgba(31, 41, 55, 0.92);
  --rb-card-border: rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.28);

  --rb-paper-bg:    #1c2230;
  --rb-paper-ink:   #e5e7eb;
  --rb-paper-rule:  rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.35);
}

/* Widen the content column to match projects + tech-zone. */
.page-columns {
  grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 4rem [screen-start-inset] 0 [page-start] 0 [page-start-inset] 0 [body-start-outset] 0 [body-start] 0 [body-content-start] 1fr [body-content-end] 0 [body-end] 0 [body-end-outset] 0 [page-end-inset] 0 [page-end] 0 [screen-end-inset] 4rem [screen-end] !important;
}
main.content.column-page { padding-left: 0 !important; padding-right: 0 !important; }

.rb-shell {
  max-width: 100%;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 3rem clamp(1rem, 2vw, 2rem) 5rem;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 2;
}

/* ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   JOURNAL MASTHEAD — distinct from the Tech Zone terminal.
   Editorial layout modeled on a journal article title block:
   double horizontal rule + publication/volume meta line,
   centered serif title, byline, and an abstract section
   bracketed by another double rule.
   ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

.rb-masthead {
  max-width: 92rem;
  margin: 0 auto 2.6rem;
  padding: 0 0 1.6rem;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
}

.rb-rule-double {
  height: 0;
  margin: 0;
  border: none;
  border-top: 3px solid var(--rb-paper-ink);
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rb-paper-ink);
  padding-top: 4px;
}

.rb-rule-double-flip {
  border-top: 1px solid var(--rb-paper-ink);
  border-bottom: 3px solid var(--rb-paper-ink);
}

.rb-rule-single {
  border: none;
  border-top: 1px solid var(--rb-paper-ink);
  margin: 1.2rem 0 1.2rem;
  opacity: 0.85;
}

.rb-meta-row {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: baseline;
  gap: 1rem;
  padding: 0.55rem 0.2rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
}

.rb-pub-name { font-weight: 700; }
.rb-pub-vol  { font-weight: 600; opacity: 0.85; }

.rb-title-block {
  text-align: center;
  padding: 2.4rem 1rem 1.6rem;
}

.rb-section-label {
  margin: 0 0 0.7rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.32em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--rb-accent);
}

.rb-headline {
  margin: 0 0 1rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: clamp(2.4rem, 5.2vw, 4.2rem);
  line-height: 1.02;
  letter-spacing: -0.015em;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
}

.rb-headline .cursor {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 0.45ch;
  margin-left: 0.15ch;
  background: var(--rb-accent);
  color: transparent;
  animation: rb-blink 1.05s steps(2, end) infinite;
}
@keyframes rb-blink { 50% { background: transparent; } }

.rb-byline {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 1.05rem;
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
}
.rb-byline em { font-style: italic; color: var(--rb-muted); font-size: 0.95em; }

.rb-abstract-block {
  max-width: 60rem;
  margin: 0 auto;
  padding: 0 1rem;
  text-align: justify;
  hyphens: auto;
}

.rb-abstract-body {
  margin: 0 0 0.8rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.65;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
}
.rb-abstract-body:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }

.rb-abstract-leader {
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--rb-accent);
  margin-right: 0.4rem;
}

.rb-abstract-body em { font-style: italic; color: var(--rb-paper-ink); }

/* ─────────────────  Section + filter strip  ───────────────── */

.rb-section-kicker {
  margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  color: var(--rb-accent);
}
.rb-section-kicker::before { content: "§ "; opacity: 0.55; font-family: var(--rb-serif); font-weight: 700; }

.rb-section-title {
  margin: 0 0 1.2rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: clamp(1.4rem, 2.4vw, 1.85rem);
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.15;
}

.rb-filter-strip {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.85rem;
  margin: 0 0 1.6rem;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}

.rb-filter-input {
  flex: 1 1 22rem;
  min-width: 14rem;
  max-width: 32rem;
  padding: 0.55rem 0.85rem 0.55rem 2.2rem;
  border: 1.5px solid var(--rb-card-border);
  border-radius: 0.7rem;
  background:
    color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-paper-bg), transparent 30%)
    url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='none' stroke='%2394a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'><circle cx='11' cy='11' r='7'/><line x1='21' y1='21' x2='16.65' y2='16.65'/></svg>") no-repeat 0.65rem center / 1rem 1rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: inherit;
  transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease;
}

.rb-filter-input:focus {
  outline: none;
  border-color: var(--rb-accent);
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--rb-accent) 22%, transparent);
}

.rb-filter-count {
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  color: var(--rb-muted);
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
}
.rb-filter-count strong { color: var(--rb-accent); font-weight: 700; }

/* ─────────────────  Card grid  ───────────────── */

.rb-card-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 1.5rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}

.rb-card {
  position: relative;
  display: block;
  border: 1.5px solid var(--rb-card-border);
  border-radius: 1.35rem;
  background: var(--rb-card-bg);
  overflow: hidden;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: inherit;
  transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
}
.rb-card:hover,
.rb-card:focus-visible {
  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--rb-accent) 38%, var(--rb-card-border));
  box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.10);
  transform: translateY(-2px);
  text-decoration: none;
  color: inherit;
}
.rb-card:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--rb-accent) 55%, transparent);
  outline-offset: 0.2rem;
}
.rb-card[hidden] { display: none; }

.rb-card-summary {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: 1.1rem;
  padding: 1.55rem;
}

.rb-card-tags {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.6rem;
}

.rb-tag {
  padding: 0.28rem 0.7rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  border: 1.2px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--rb-accent) 35%, var(--rb-card-border));
  color: var(--rb-accent);
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-accent), transparent 90%);
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.74rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.rb-tag::before { content: "#"; opacity: 0.6; margin-right: 0.05em; }

.rb-card-name-row {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.85rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
}

.rb-card-number {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  width: 2rem; height: 2rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-accent), transparent 82%);
  color: var(--rb-accent);
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

.rb-card-name {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 1.3rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.2;
}

.rb-card-meta {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.75rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.74rem;
  color: var(--rb-muted);
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.rb-card-date::before { content: "[ "; opacity: 0.55; }
.rb-card-date::after  { content: " ]"; opacity: 0.55; }

.rb-status-pill {
  padding: 0.18rem 0.55rem;
  border-radius: 999px;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  border: 1px solid currentColor;
}
.rb-status-pill.published { color: var(--rb-accent); background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-accent), transparent 92%); }
.rb-status-pill.wip       { color: var(--rb-warn);   background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-warn),   transparent 92%); }
.rb-status-pill.todo      { color: var(--rb-muted);  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-muted),  transparent 92%); }

.rb-card-preview-copy {
  margin: 0;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.rb-card-cta {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.4rem;
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--rb-accent);
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
  transition: gap 0.18s ease;
}
.rb-card:hover .rb-card-cta,
.rb-card:focus-visible .rb-card-cta { gap: 0.7rem; }
.rb-card-cta .arrow { transition: transform 0.18s ease; }
.rb-card:hover .rb-card-cta .arrow,
.rb-card:focus-visible .rb-card-cta .arrow { transform: translateX(2px); }

/* ────  Preview panel: paper tear-sheet (used for ALL cards)  ──── */

.rb-paper {
  background: var(--rb-paper-bg);
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
  border: 1px solid var(--rb-paper-rule);
  border-radius: 0.75rem;
  overflow: hidden;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  position: relative;
  background-image:
    linear-gradient(90deg, transparent 0, transparent 49.9%, color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-paper-rule), transparent 80%) 50%, transparent 50.1%, transparent 100%);
}

.rb-paper-stamp {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 0.5rem;
  padding: 0.45rem 0.85rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rb-paper-rule);
  background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--rb-paper-bg), #000 4%);
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  color: var(--rb-muted);
  letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.rb-paper-stamp .section { color: var(--rb-accent); font-weight: 700; }
.rb-paper-stamp .date   { margin-left: auto; opacity: 0.8; }

.rb-paper-body { padding: 1rem 1.1rem 1.1rem; }

.rb-paper-title {
  margin: 0 0 0.35rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 1.1rem;
  line-height: 1.22;
  text-align: center;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
}

.rb-paper-authors {
  margin: 0 0 0.75rem;
  text-align: center;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-style: italic;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  color: var(--rb-muted);
}

.rb-paper-abstract-label {
  display: block;
  text-align: center;
  font-family: var(--rb-mono);
  font-size: 0.65rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
  margin: 0 0 0.3rem;
}

.rb-paper-abstract-rule {
  border: none;
  height: 1px;
  background: var(--rb-paper-rule);
  margin: 0 4rem 0.55rem;
}

.rb-paper-abstract {
  margin: 0 0 0.6rem;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
  line-height: 1.55;
  color: var(--rb-paper-ink);
  text-align: justify;
  hyphens: auto;
}

.rb-paper-fig {
  margin: 0;
  font-family: var(--rb-serif);
  font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-style: italic;
  color: var(--rb-muted);
  text-align: center;
}

/* ─────────────────  Responsive  ───────────────── */

@media (max-width: 1100px) {
  .rb-card-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
}

@media (max-width: 640px) {
  .page-columns {
    grid-template-columns: [screen-start] 0.5rem [screen-start-inset] 0 [page-start] 0 [page-start-inset] 0 [body-start-outset] 0 [body-start] 0 [body-content-start] 1fr [body-content-end] 0 [body-end] 0 [body-end-outset] 0 [page-end-inset] 0 [page-end] 0 [screen-end-inset] 0.5rem [screen-end] !important;
  }
  .rb-shell        { padding: 2.5rem 0.75rem 4rem; }
  .rb-title-block  { padding: 1.8rem 0.5rem 1.2rem; }
  .rb-meta-row     { font-size: 0.62rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; gap: 0.2rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.2rem; }
  .rb-card-summary { padding: 1.25rem; }
  .rb-paper-body   { font-size: 0.92rem; }
}
</style>

<div class="rb-shell">

  <!-- ───────  Page lead-in (above the masthead)  ─────── -->
  <p class="rb-section-kicker">Notes &amp; tutorials</p>
  <h2 class="rb-section-title">Posts, in roughly the order they matured.</h2>

  <!-- ───────  Journal-masthead header  ─────── -->
  <header class="rb-masthead" role="banner">
    <hr class="rb-rule-double rb-rule-top"/>
    <div class="rb-meta-row">
      <span class="rb-pub-name">Journal of evannsmc</span>
      <span class="rb-pub-vol">Vol. 1 · 2026 · Notes &amp; Tutorials</span>
    </div>
    <hr class="rb-rule-double rb-rule-double-flip"/>

    <div class="rb-title-block">
      <p class="rb-section-label">Research Notes &middot; Tutorials &middot; Working Drafts</p>
      <h1 class="rb-headline">Research Blog</h1>
      <p class="rb-byline">
        Evanns Morales-Cuadrado
      </p>
    </div>

    <hr class="rb-rule-single"/>

    <div class="rb-abstract-block">
      <p class="rb-abstract-body">
        <span class="rb-abstract-leader">Abstract.</span>
        Explanatory pieces on robotics, control theory, optimal control, JAX, and lessons from hardware
        experiments — topics I wish had been explained to me in a particular manner, working notes for
        ongoing research threads, and small standalone tutorials I keep returning to.
      </p>
      <p class="rb-abstract-body">
        <span class="rb-abstract-leader">Current.</span>
        Finished writeups on Newton-Raphson flow tracking control and quadrotor dynamics; in-progress notes
        on PX4 + ROS&nbsp;2 offboard control; and a stack of <em>"Understanding ___"</em> skeletons —
        game-theoretic control, JAX, optimal control, reachability, reinforcement learning — to be filled
        in as I work through each one.
      </p>
    </div>

    <hr class="rb-rule-double rb-rule-bottom"/>
  </header>

  <!-- ───────  Inline filter strip  ─────── -->
  <div class="rb-filter-strip">
    <input
      class="rb-filter-input"
      type="search"
      placeholder="filter by title, topic, or #tag…"
      aria-label="Filter posts"
      autocomplete="off"
    />
    <span class="rb-filter-count" aria-live="polite">
      showing <strong id="rb-count-shown">8</strong> of <strong>8</strong>
    </span>
  </div>

  <!-- ───────  Card grid (all paper-style previews)  ─────── -->
  <div class="rb-card-grid" id="rb-card-grid">

    <!-- 01 — Understanding PX4 (in progress) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-10-15-UnderstandingPX4/"
       data-status="wip" data-tags="px4 quadrotor ros2 hardware tools software implementation"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding PX4 Autopilot and Offboard Control via ROS2">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">px4</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">quadrotor</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">ros2</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">hardware</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">implementation</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">01</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding PX4 Autopilot and Offboard Control via ROS 2</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">2025-10-15</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill wip">in progress</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          Working notes on the PX4 flight stack and how to drive it from ROS 2 for offboard control with
          novel algorithms — plus a tour of my open-source modular PX4-ROS 2 offboard package and custom
          logger.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Working Notes</span>
            <span class="date">2025-10-15</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding PX4 Autopilot and Offboard Control via ROS&nbsp;2</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · 2025</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              We document the PX4 flight stack and the bridge from a companion-computer ROS&nbsp;2 graph
              into onboard control: uXRCE-DDS message flow, offboard-mode entry conditions, and a modular
              offboard package for evaluating novel control algorithms on real hardware.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · ROS&nbsp;2 ↔ PX4 message flow</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">read the working notes <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 02 — Understanding Quadrotor Dynamics (published) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-09-29-QuadrotorDynamics/"
       data-status="published" data-tags="dynamical systems control theory quadrotor geometric control modeling"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding Quadrotor Dynamics and Control">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">dynamical systems</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">quadrotor</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">geometric control</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">modeling</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">02</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding Quadrotor Dynamics and Control</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">2025-09-29</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill published">published</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          A practical overview of modeling, coordinate frames, and control-relevant dynamics of the quadrotor
          UAV — from Newton-Euler equations to configuration on SE(3) and why geometric controllers sidestep
          Euler singularities.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Research Note</span>
            <span class="date">2025-09-29</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding Quadrotor Dynamics and Control</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · 2025</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              We derive the rigid-body dynamics of a quadrotor UAV via Newton-Euler mechanics, lift the
              configuration onto SE(3), and motivate geometric controllers over Euler-angle parameterisations
              by showing how singularities arise and are sidestepped on the manifold.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · body frame &amp; rotor geometry on SE(3)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">read the writeup <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 03 — Newton-Raphson Flow (published) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-05-22-NewtonRaphsonFlow/"
       data-status="published" data-tags="control theory iterative methods dynamical systems newton-raphson"
       aria-label="Read: Newton-Raphson Flow — From Iteration to Continuous Control">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">iterative methods</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">dynamical systems</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">newton-raphson</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">03</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Newton-Raphson Flow: From Iteration to Continuous Control</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">2025-05-22</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill published">published</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          The Newton-Raphson flow tracking controller — its derivation from the iterative root-finding
          method, its adaptation for dynamical systems via output prediction, and why it stays light enough
          to deploy onboard a real quadrotor.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Research Note</span>
            <span class="date">2025-05-22</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Newton-Raphson Flow:<br/>From Iteration to Continuous Control</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · 2025</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              We reframe reference tracking as a continuous-time Newton-Raphson root-finding problem. The
              iterative update law is adapted for dynamical systems via output prediction, yielding a
              controller that is computationally lightweight, easy to retune, and demonstrably deployable
              onboard a quadrotor UAV.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · prediction–correction loop</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">read the paper-style note <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 04 — Understanding Game-Theoretic Control (TODO) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-11-11-GameTheoreticControl/"
       data-status="todo" data-tags="dynamical systems control theory game theory differential games"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding Game-Theoretic Control">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">game theory</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">differential games</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">04</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding Game-Theoretic Control</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">planned</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill todo">todo</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          A future writeup on differential games as a lens on control: open-loop vs. feedback Nash
          equilibria, and the Isaacs equation as a minimax cousin of Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Planned Note</span>
            <span class="date">TBD</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding Game-Theoretic Control</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · forthcoming</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              <em>Forthcoming.</em> Differential games as a lens on control: open-loop vs. feedback Nash
              equilibria, who knows what and when, and the Isaacs equation as the minimax cousin of
              Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman. We work a two-player toy example before stating the general result.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · pursuit–evasion phase portrait (planned)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">peek at the scratchpad <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 05 — Understanding JAX (TODO) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-11-11-UnderstandingJax/"
       data-status="todo" data-tags="tools software jax implementation"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding JAX">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">jax</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">tools</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">software</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">implementation</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">05</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding JAX for Control Research</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">planned</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill todo">todo</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          The case for JAX in control research: how <code>jit</code>, <code>grad</code>, and <code>vmap</code>
          turn controllers into differentiable, vectorisable, fast-by-default functions.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Planned Note</span>
            <span class="date">TBD</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding JAX for Control Research</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · forthcoming</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              <em>Forthcoming.</em> The case for JAX in control research: how <em>jit</em>, <em>grad</em>,
              and <em>vmap</em> turn controllers into differentiable, vectorisable, fast-by-default
              functions, and how PyTree state aligns with treating control laws as pure functions of
              parameters and observations.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · jit-compiled NMPC inner loop (planned)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">peek at the scratchpad <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 06 — Understanding Optimal Control & CoV (TODO) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-11-11-UnderstandingOptimalControl/"
       data-status="todo" data-tags="optimal control calculus of variations control theory pontryagin hjb"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding Optimal Control and Calculus of Variations">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">optimal control</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">calculus of variations</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">06</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding Optimal Control &amp; Calculus of Variations</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">planned</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill todo">todo</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          The bridge from calculus of variations to Pontryagin and Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman — and why all
          three are saying the same thing about minimising a cost functional along a trajectory.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Planned Note</span>
            <span class="date">TBD</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding Optimal Control &amp; Calculus of Variations</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · forthcoming</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              <em>Forthcoming.</em> We follow the thread from calculus of variations to Pontryagin's
              maximum principle and Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman, showing how the principle of optimality glues
              the local-necessary-condition viewpoint to the global value-function viewpoint.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · CoV → PMP → HJB (planned)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">peek at the scratchpad <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 07 — Understanding Reachability (TODO) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-11-11-UnderstandingReachability/"
       data-status="todo" data-tags="reachability dynamical systems control theory safety"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding Reachability">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">reachability</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">dynamical systems</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">safety</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">07</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding Reachability Analysis</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">planned</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill todo">todo</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          What the reachable set actually is, why forward and backward reach answer different safety
          questions, and where mixed-monotone and interval methods fit in the picture.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Planned Note</span>
            <span class="date">TBD</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding Reachability Analysis</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · forthcoming</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              <em>Forthcoming.</em> What the reachable set is, why forward and backward reach answer
              different safety questions, and how mixed-monotone and interval methods give sound, fast
              inclusions that are useful in practice for runtime verification of controllers.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · forward vs backward reach tubes (planned)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">peek at the scratchpad <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

    <!-- 08 — Understanding RL (TODO) -->
    <a class="rb-card" href="./posts/2025-11-11-UnderstandingRL/"
       data-status="todo" data-tags="reinforcement learning control theory dynamical systems bellman"
       aria-label="Read: Understanding Reinforcement Learning">
      <div class="rb-card-summary">
        <div class="rb-card-tags">
          <span class="rb-tag">reinforcement learning</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">control theory</span>
          <span class="rb-tag">dynamical systems</span>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-name-row">
          <span class="rb-card-number">08</span>
          <h3 class="rb-card-name">Understanding Reinforcement Learning</h3>
        </div>
        <div class="rb-card-meta">
          <span class="rb-card-date">planned</span>
          <span class="rb-status-pill todo">todo</span>
        </div>
        <p class="rb-card-preview-copy">
          Treating RL as control under uncertainty: Bellman as the fixed-point operator, TD vs. Monte-Carlo
          as stochastic-approximation cousins, and why policy gradient is just calculus on expectations.
        </p>

        <div class="rb-paper" aria-hidden="true">
          <div class="rb-paper-stamp">
            <span class="section">Planned Note</span>
            <span class="date">TBD</span>
          </div>
          <div class="rb-paper-body">
            <p class="rb-paper-title">Understanding Reinforcement Learning</p>
            <p class="rb-paper-authors">E. Morales-Cuadrado · forthcoming</p>
            <span class="rb-paper-abstract-label">Abstract</span>
            <hr class="rb-paper-abstract-rule"/>
            <p class="rb-paper-abstract">
              <em>Forthcoming.</em> Treating RL as control under uncertainty: Bellman as the fixed-point
              operator, TD vs. Monte-Carlo as stochastic-approximation cousins, and policy gradient as
              calculus on expectations. We emphasise the dynamical-systems framing rather than the AI
              framing.
            </p>
            <p class="rb-paper-fig">Fig. 1 · value iteration as fixed-point flow (planned)</p>
          </div>
        </div>

        <span class="rb-card-cta">peek at the scratchpad <span class="arrow">→</span></span>
      </div>
    </a>

  </div>

</div>

<script>
// Lightweight live filter across the hand-built blog cards.
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
  var input = document.querySelector('.rb-filter-input');
  var grid  = document.getElementById('rb-card-grid');
  var count = document.getElementById('rb-count-shown');
  if (!input || !grid || !count) return;

  var cards = Array.prototype.slice.call(grid.querySelectorAll('.rb-card'));

  function applyFilter() {
    var q = input.value.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/^#/, '');
    var shown = 0;
    cards.forEach(function (card) {
      if (!q) { card.hidden = false; shown++; return; }
      var title = (card.querySelector('.rb-card-name') || {}).textContent || '';
      var tags  = card.getAttribute('data-tags') || '';
      var copy  = (card.querySelector('.rb-card-preview-copy') || {}).textContent || '';
      var hay = (title + ' ' + tags + ' ' + copy).toLowerCase();
      var match = hay.indexOf(q) !== -1;
      card.hidden = !match;
      if (match) shown++;
    });
    count.textContent = String(shown);
  }

  input.addEventListener('input', applyFilter);
});
</script>
```
